lesson.title: Exponential Smoothing
lesson.body: Exponential smoothing forecasts by weighting recent observations more heavily. Simple exponential smoothing: ŝ_t = αx_t + (1−α)ŝ_{t-1} where 0 < α < 1. Holt's two-parameter method adds a trend component. Holt-Winters (triple exponential smoothing) adds seasonality. The smoothing parameter α controls how quickly old data is forgotten.

rooms.question: In simple exponential smoothing ŝ_t = αx_t + (1−α)ŝ_{t-1}, larger α means:
answers: More weight on recent observations, faster adaptation
More weight on older observations, slower adaptation
rooms.explanation: α close to 1 weights the current observation heavily (fast adaptation). α close to 0 averages over many past values (slow adaptation).

rooms.question: Simple exponential smoothing is appropriate for series with:
rooms.explanation: Simple (single) ES handles level but not trend or seasonality. Holt's method handles trend; Holt-Winters handles both.

rooms.question: Holt's method has two smoothing parameters α and β. β controls:
rooms.explanation: Holt's method: L_t = level, T_t = trend. α smooths the level, β smooths the trend. The h-step forecast is L_t + h·T_t.

rooms.question: Order the components of the Holt-Winters additive model:
rooms.explanation: Holt-Winters: update level, update trend, update seasonal factor, combine for forecast.

rooms.question: The k-step-ahead forecast from simple exponential smoothing is:
answers: ŝ_T for all h (the last smoothed value)
rooms.explanation: Simple ES h-step forecast is just the last smoothed value ŝ_T for all horizons h ≥ 1 — a flat forecast (appropriate when no trend).

boss.name: The Forecast Wraith
boss.taunt: The future is an echo of the present. How heavily do you weight the past?

questions.question: The optimal α for exponential smoothing is found by:
answers: Minimizing SSE on the training data
questions.explanation: α is typically estimated by minimizing the sum of squared one-step-ahead forecast errors on the training data.

questions.question: Exponential smoothing is equivalent to ARIMA(0,1,1) with:
questions.explanation: SES is exactly ARIMA(0,1,1) with MA parameter θ = 1−α. This equivalence allows using ARIMA theory for ES.

questions.question: For a monthly series with strong yearly seasonality and upward trend, which model is most appropriate?
questions.explanation: Holt-Winters (triple ES) handles both trend and seasonality. With monthly data and annual cycles, s=12.
